zh.lifeSummary: George J. Reichley於1792年出生在美國賓夕法尼亞州盧爾納普萊姆斯 堂史普。他和Magdalene Rayl至少有5個兒子和1個女兒。他於1860年住在美國俄亥俄州素米特諾頓 堂史普。他去世於1866年12月15日在美國印第安納州馬歇爾普萊姆斯享年74歲。

nameMeanings.name: Reichley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Reichle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Albrecht, Claus, Fritz, Hans, Otto, Wieland.

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

historicEvents.title: Creating the Eleventh Amendment
historicEvents.description: The Eleventh Amendment restricts the ability of any people to start a lawsuit against the states in federal court.
Ohio was the first state admitted to the Union from the Northwest Territory.
War of 1812. U.S. declares war on Britain over British interference with American maritime shipping and westward expansion.

historicEvents.title: Harrisburg Becomes the State Capital
historicEvents.description: Harrisburg had important parts with migration, the Civil War, and the Industrial Revolution.
historicEvents.urlTitle: United States History-History of Harrisburg Pennsylvania

historicEvents.title: The Black Hawk War
historicEvents.description: Convinced that a group of Native American tribes were hostile, The United States formed a frontier militia to stop them in their tracks. Even though Black Hawk was hoping to avoid bloodshed while trying to resettle on tribal land, U.S. officials opened fire on the Native Americans. Black Hawk then responded to this confrontation by successfully attacking the militia at the Battle of Stillman's Run and then left northward. After a few months the militia caught up with Black Hawk and his men and defeated them at the Battle of Wisconsin Heights. While being weakened by hunger, injuries and desertion, Black Hawk and the rest of the many native survivors retreated towards the Mississippi. Unfortunately, Black Hawk and other leaders were later captured when they surrendered to the US forces and were then imprisoned for a year.

In 1834, Colonel William Polk, James Blair, and John Sering laid out the town of Plymouth along the Yellow River at the intersection of the Michigan Road and the old La Porte trail. Marshall County was divided out of St. Joseph County in 1836 and Plymouth was selected as the county seat.

historicEvents.title: Constitution of 1851
historicEvents.description: Due to the state’s financial crisis during the previous decade and growing criticism toward state government. Voters approve the Constitution of 1851 which forbade the state government from going into debt.
Abraham Lincoln issues Emancipation Proclamation, declaring slaves in Confederate states to be free.

en.lifeSummary: George J. Reichley was born in 1792, in Plymouth Township, Luzerne, Pennsylvania, United States. He had at least 5 sons and 1 daughter with Magdalene Rayl. He lived in Norton Township, Summit, Ohio, United States in 1860. He died on 15 December 1866, in Plymouth, Marshall, Indiana, United States, at the age of 74.

ja.lifeSummary: George J. Reichleyは1792年にアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州ルズエルンプリモウス トウンシュイップで生まれた。彼はMagdalene Raylとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1860年にはアメリカ合衆国オハイオ州スンミトノートン トウンシュイップに住んでいた。彼は1866年12月15日にアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州マーシャルプリモウスで74歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: George J. Reichley(은)는 1792년 미국 펜실베이니아 주 루즈어니 플림우스 타운시프에서 태어났다. 그는 Magdalene Rayl(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1860년도에 미국 오하이오 주 수미 노르턴 타운시프에서 살았다. 그는 1866년 12월 15일 미국 인디애나 주 마샤 플림우스에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: George J. Reichley родился в 1792 г. в местности плимоут тоуншип, лузирн, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь от Magdalene Rayl. Он жил в местности нортон тоуншип, суммит, Огайо, Соединенные штаты в 1860 году. Он умер 15 декабря 1866 г. в местности плимоут, маршолл, Индиана, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 74 лет.

es.lifeSummary: George J. Reichley nació en 1792, en Plymouth Township, Luzerne, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija con Magdalene Rayl. Vivió en Norton Township, Summit, Ohio, Estados Unidos en 1860. Murió el 15 de diciembre de 1866, en Plymouth, Marshall, Indiana, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 74 años.
